Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Okay I need to carefully analyze this process event log from a financial services support workflow. The user wants me to rename activities that appear multiple times in the same case to reflect their contextual differences. Let me start by understanding the task fully.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the log I see multiple cases C001 C002 C003 each with several activities. The same activity names like Verify Documents and Call Customer appear multiple times within single cases but their purposes differ based on timing and context.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I should identify which activities recur. In C001 Verify Documents appears twice first when checking initial documents then after the customer submits additional ones. Similarly Call Customer in C001 is to request missing documents while in C002 its first to clarify an account discrepancy then to confirm adjustments. In C003 Call Customer is to ask the customer to return to the branch.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user needs clear context specific names. Ill rename based on the Additional Context clues and position in the process. For example the first Verify Documents in each case is initial verification while the second is re verification after more information comes in. Call Customer varies too sometimes its to request info sometimes to clarify sometimes to confirm changes or even to have someone return.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to ensure the new names are descriptive yet concise. Let me go case by case and draft appropriate names. Then Ill present the revised log with justifications for each change. The goal is to make the workflow clearer by disambiguating similar activities through their specific roles in the process.
